item.title: Laundering cases in PNG enforce historic round log export ban
item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Meet some of Papua New Guinea’s most innovative large businesses
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A record number of corporations entered the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards this year, with the shortlists announced earlier this month in Port Moresby. From a digital platform for strata titles to a system to streamline coffee exports, we consider the five projects shortlisted for this year’s corporate

item.title: JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Government of Japan, through the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA), has joined the Government of Papua New Guinea and the National Fisheries Authority to The post JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nobel Laureate Dr. José Ramos-Horta visits Port Moresby, backing PNG’s eco-leadership and inviting PNG musicians to Timor-Leste. A new era for Melanesian ties. The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and

item.title: PNG's Central Police Mobility Boosted With New Vehicle for Biura Post
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] A new police vehicle has been handed over to the Biura Police Post in Central Province as part of ongoing support from the Hiri-Koiari District Development Authority. The presentation ceremony, attended by community leaders and police officers, marked the fourth vehicle donated by the district autho

item.title: The Power of the Kina
item.description: [Pawa TV] Over the past decade, Papua New Guinea's kina has faced significant depreciation due to reforms and global conditions, impacting purchasing power and increasing import costs, despite attempts to boost economic stability. The post The Power of the Kina first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Job Seekers to Meet Australian Employers at PALM Expo
item.description: [PNG Facts] Hundreds of Papua New Guineans seeking overseas employment opportunities are expected to benefit this week as Australian employers arrive in the country to recruit workers under the Pacific Australia Labour Mobility (PALM) scheme. A total of 23 approved employers from Australia are in Papua New Guin

item.title: Full scale emergency response to PX Flight 9606 incident
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Airports Corporation (NAC) confirms that a full-scale emergency response was activated this week on Monday 18th May 2026 at the Port Moresby International Airport following an undercarriage issue experienced by an Air Niugini Q400 aircraft operating from Port Moresby to Moro, in the Sou

item.title: Prime Minister Marape defends PNG LNG landowner equity structure
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister James Marape has defended the Government’s restructuring of the 4.27 percent PNG LNG equity benefit in Parliament. He was responding to questions from Hiri-Koiari MP Keith Iduhu, with supplementary questions from former Prime Minister Peter O’Neill. He assured Parliament that all stak

item.title: Hiri-Koiari DDA donates fourth police vehicle in Central Province
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Hiri-Koiari District Development Authority has donated a fourth vehicle to police in Central Province, with the latest unit handed over to the Biura Police Post to improve policing along the Hiri West coastline. Central Provincial Police Commander Chief Inspector Joseph Salle in a statement said

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: Review: Summit Bar, Hilton Port Moresby
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Robert Hamilton-Jones returns to the rooftop Summit Bar in the Hilton Port Moresby to check out their cocktail range. The post Review: Summit Bar, Hilton Port Moresby appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae
item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ryan Ani: From Raukele to the Barramandis
item.description: [Inside PNG] A fresh face is joining the PNG Barramandis as they head to Japan for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific (EAP) Qualifier. At just 22 years old, Ryan Ani is ready to make his international debut. Ani, who hails from Hula and Babaka in the Rigo Coastal area of Central Province, grew up [...

item.title: PNG Chiefs Strengthen Coaching Staff with Luke Burt Appointment
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The PNG Chiefs have continued building towards their 2028 entry by securing former NRL star Luke Burt as an assistant coach from November 2027. The appointment adds more experience to the club’s growing football department as preparations intensify ahead of the inaugural season. Burt arrives with a

item.title: PNG’s Fastest Men
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's sprinters, notably Pais Wisil and Tovetuna Tuna, have set national records, inspiring future athletes. The country's athletics scene is thriving, raising questions about increased support for talent development. The post PNG’s Fastest Men first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Social Media is Changing the Face of the Beauty Industry
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Mothers Forced to Give Birth on Hospital Floors as Labour Ward Crisis Deepens
item.description: [PNG Facts] Women arriving to deliver babies at the country’s biggest referral hospital are now being forced onto floors as overcrowding at the maternity ward reaches breaking point in Port Moresby. At the centre of the growing crisis is the labour ward at the Port Moresby General Hospital where doctors say pat

item.title: Church Leader Condemns Brutal SARV Killing
item.description: [Post Courier] The Head Bishop of the Evangelical Lutheran Church of Papua New Guinea, Rev. Dr. Jack Urame, has strongly condemned the latest sorcery accusation related violence (SARV) killing, describing the act as barbaric, inhuman and a major threat to peace and stability in Papua New Guinea. In a powerful publ

item.title: Sir Puka raises concerns over delay of Papua LNG Project
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Member for Abau, Sir Puka Temu has raised serious concerns over delays surrounding the Papua LNG Project. Speaking in Parliament, Sir Puka said there had been little to no update from TotalEnergies following Prime Minister James Marape’s recent visit to the country where the company is based. Sir Pu

item.title: Health Minister condemns Medical Board over nine-month delay in registering oveseas-triained doctor
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Health Minister Elias Kapavore has criticized the PNG Medical Board over a nine-month delay in registering a Papua New Guinean doctor trained overseas, saying the country cannot afford hold-ups while facing a shortage of medical professionals. Minister Kapavore said instructions had already been

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: The other LEO satellite provider: Papua New Guinea business begin trials of OneWeb
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] While Starlink was approved for use in PNG in late April to much fanfare, the approval of another LEO service – OneWeb – has received relatively little attention. Robbie Huxley, Managing Director of TE PNG, tells Business Advantage PNG why OneWeb’s arrival is such an important moment for Papua New G

item.title: USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG icon Alex Johnston signs with PNG Chiefs for 2028. The NRL try-scoring king joins as the first local hero, honoring his proud Kumuls heritage. The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG C

item.title: Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwide
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Motorists across Papua New Guinea are seeing relief at fuel stations after the Independent Consumer and Competition Commission (ICCC) confirmed that retail fuel prices have dropped following the National Government’s subsidy intervention. Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwi

item.title: Carbon Trade in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea can leverage carbon trading through its rich ecosystems to generate income, support local communities, and meet its emissions reduction targets while ensuring transparency and protection for landowners in this evolving market. The post Carbon Trade in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Air Plane Stops Suddenly on Goroka Runway to Avoid Bird Strike
item.description: [PNG Facts] A routine PNG Air service turned into an emergency stop at Goroka Airport on Monday after a swarm of birds moved across the runway moments before takeoff. The pilot of the ATR 72-600 reacted immediately, aborting the departure while the aircraft was still on the ground and bringing the plane to a ha

item.title: PC Online Tidbits
item.description: [Post Courier] As we all wait today for the kickoff to Game 1 of the 2026 State of Origin Series taking place this evening, we take queue from a story, where during Cyclone Guba, Australian soldiers assisted during the humanitarian relief response in the Northern Province. One soldier spoke to a local, and it was

item.title: Iduhu questions Exclusion of Central Province Landowners from PNGLNG Equity SPV Board
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Koiari MP Keith Iduhu has questioned the government’s decision to exclude Central Province and plant-site landowners from direct representation on the board of the Kroton Equity Options Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV), which manages a 4.27 percent equity stake in the PNGLNG Project. Speaking in Parlia

item.title: France, EU back K400 million Rabaul Port redevelopment
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea has secured more than K400 million in funding support from France and European investment partners to redevelop and modernize Rabaul Port in East New Britain Province. Speaking yesterday morning at Melanesian Haus in Port Moresby after returning from

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Papua New Guinean manufacturers ‘have increased their investment in the country’
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Most Papua New Guinean manufacturers are planning to increase their capital expenditure this year, according to the latest PNG 100 CEO Survey. In this deep dive, sector leaders outline their growth strategies to Business Advantage PNG. The post Papua New Guinean manufacturers ‘have increased their i

item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank backs the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards, sponsoring the Small Business category to drive growth and recognize local creative entrepreneurs. The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 ap

item.title: PNG Hunters fall to Souths Logan Magpies at Santos Stadium
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] A fast-starting Souths Logan Magpies outfit spoiled the PNG Hunters’ home appearance in Port Moresby after claiming a 30-20 victory in the Hostplus Cup on Sunday evening. The Hunters showed flashes of brilliance in attack but defensive lapses proved costly as the Queensland visitors capitalised on t

item.title: Matrilineal & Patrilineal Societies in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's societal structures include matrilineal and patrilineal systems, influencing descent, land rights, and gender roles, with matrilineal communities empowering women through inheritance and social influence, and patrilineal focusing on male lineage. The post Matrilineal & Patrilineal

These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG's Air Niugini Expands Presence in East Sepik With New Wewak Office
item.description: [PNG Facts] Air Niugini has officially opened a new sales office in Wewak, marking a major expansion of its customer services in East Sepik Province. The new office, launched on Thursday at the newly developed Tangmow Plaza in the centre of Wewak township, is expected to improve access to airline services for p

item.title: CBM Conducted Women’s Empowerment Integrated Outreach
item.description: [Post Courier] Christian Blind Mission (CBM) recently held a women empowerment integrated outreach program at Six Mile in the Asaro Watabung District of Eastern Highlands Province. This event was organized in conjunction with the Mini Federation hosted by Adventist Community Service (ACS). As a longstanding partne

item.title: State Ministers urged to confirm Kagua-Erave DDA leadership
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Kagua-Erave Community leader Nicholas Sambu is calling on Provincial & Local Level Government Affairs Minister Soroi Eoe and Public Service Minister Joe Sungi to urgently confirm and publicly declare the legitimate Acting Chairman and CEO/District Administrator for Kagua-Erave District. Mr. Sambu sa

item.title: 20 Enga police officers complete defensive tactics training
item.description: [The PNG Sun] 20 police officers from Enga Province have completed defensive tactics training at the Kimininga Police Barracks in Mt Hagen as police respond to increasing attacks on officers in the province. A second group of 15 officers is currently undergoing the same training program, which is supported by the

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank reopens its upgraded Rangeview Digital Hub on Level 1, bringing instant cards, loans, and digital support to Port Moresby shoppers. item.title: NZ Police Commissioner Visits PNG to Discuss Regional Policing Partnership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] New Zealand Police Commissioner Richard Chambers has completed a working visit to Papua New Guinea aimed at strengthening policing cooperation and regional security partnerships between the two countries. NZ Police Commissioner Visits PNG to Discuss Regional Policing Partnership/Photo supplied While

item.title: Disaster Preparedness Plans in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea is highly vulnerable to natural disasters due to its geography. Community-based disaster preparedness plans are being implemented, involving local leaders and groups to enhance response and reduce risks. The post Disaster Preparedness Plans in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Visits ATR Aircraft Factory in France
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Prime Minister James Marape visited the ATR aircraft manufacturing facility in Toulouse, France, on Thursday to inspect new aircraft being built for Western Province landowners as part of PNG Air’s growing fleet expansion program. The visit comes as PNG Air continues increasing its ATR operation

item.description: [Post Courier] As a former series-winning Queensland Maroons centre, Josh Hannay knows what it takes to find success in the Origin arena. The post ‘He deserves his shot’: Fresh face Fifita ready to fire appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: PNG Tribal Foundation clarifies PNG’s legal framework on SARV and the Glassman Law
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Papua New Guinea Tribal Foundation wishes to clarify the current legal landscape governing sorcery-accusation related violence (SARV) in Papua New Guinea, following recent media commentary calling for a review of the repealed Sorcery Act. President of PNG Tribal Foundation GT Bustin said that ba

item.title: PM Marape calls for strong 2027 election preparation as Parliament pushes to pass outstanding Laws
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape says the Government will use the remaining parliamentary sittings before the 2027 National General Election to pass pending legislation and complete outstanding government business. Mr. Marape told a press conference at Melanesian Haus in Port Moresby yesterday mornin

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial SABL land leases.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

Meanwhile, the shortlists for the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards have been revealed - and they include exciting small and medium businesses from around the c

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: Vladimir Putin to Visit China for Strategic Talks with Xi Jinping
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Russian President Vladimir Putin is set to travel to China next week for high-level talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, as Moscow and Beijing continue to reinforce ties amid growing friction with the West. The Kremlin said Putin’s state visit will take place from May 19 to 20 and will focus on exp

item.title: Greenwashing
item.description: [Pawa TV] Greenwashing misleads the public about environmental responsibility, hinders effective climate action, and damages trust in genuine sustainability efforts, necessitating greater transparency and standardisation in environmental claims. The post Greenwashing first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG's New Ireland Seeks Greater Control in Mining Benefit from Simberi and Lihir gold mines
item.description: [PNG Facts] Governor Byron Chan Fresh negotiations are underway in New Ireland as provincial leaders push for increased benefits from the Simberi and Lihir gold mines, including higher royalty payments, equity participation and stronger development funding for local communities. Governor Byron Chan confirmed th

item.title: Historic drilling begins for Panguna
item.description: [Post Courier] A HISTORIC drilling program for exploration in Panguna was launched today. After repeated heated negotiations, protest, and media attacks, all stakeholders have now focused on redeveloping the Panguna mine which was forcefully shut down by landowners in 1989, which also ignited the 10-year Bougainvi

item.title: Goroka Airport temporarily closed
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Goroka Airport was temporarily closed on Monday following an aircraft incident involving a PNG Air ATR 72-600 during take-off.The National Airports Corporation (NAC) in a statement confirmed that the incident occurred at approximately 10:16am on Runway 17R, when the aircraft experienced a tire burst

item.title: New NIDC board tasked with reviving economic projects in New Ireland
item.description: [The PNG Sun] New Ireland Governor Byron Chan says a newly appointed board of the New Ireland Development Corporation has been tasked with improving governance and progressing stalled economic projects in the province. Speaking after the board’s swearing-in in Kavieng last month, Mr. Chan said the Provincial Exec

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: ACT NOW! Spotlights Illegal Logging at Asia Pacific Global Dialogue, Malaysia
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! recently attended and presented on illegal logging and forest crime in Papua New Guinea at the Asia- Pacific Regional Private Sector Dialogue meeting held in Malaysia.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] CEO steps down at Digicel PNG, new chair for CPL Group, new CFO at Kina Bank, first Solomon Islands country head for Remington Group, CEO appointed at PNG FM, new partner at Dentons. The post People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons appeared first on Business Adv

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend
item.description: [Inside PNG] Christchurch's Super Round rebirth will give rugby a timely boost as rival destinations, including Fiji, jostle for a piece of the action. The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend

item.title: China Hands Over US$1 Million Aid Package to PNG Government
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG has been boosted with a US$1 million humanitarian assistance package from China as both nations reaffirmed their long-standing diplomatic partnership during a ceremony in Port Moresby. China Hands Over US$1 Million Aid Package to PNG Government/Photo supplied Chinese Embassy Chargé d'affaires a.

item.title: The Malagan Art
item.description: [Pawa TV] Malagan is a ceremonial art tradition from Papua New Guinea, symbolising ancestral spirits and clan identities. Its increasing commercialisation raises concerns over cultural respect, authenticity, and spiritual significance. The post The Malagan Art first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Experience The Great Wall of China on a Layover
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Landowners Take Flight with First-Ever Commercial Aircraft Purchase
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has entered a new chapter in aviation history after landowner companies connected to the Ok Tedi mine purchased three brand-new ATR 42-600 aircraft, marking the first time local resource landowners have directly owned commercial passenger aircraft in the country. The aircraft were a

item.title: Sir Puka calls on Petroleum Minister for Oil Refinery Policy
item.description: [Post Courier] Member for Abau, Sir Puka Temu has called on the Minister for Petroleum, Jimmy Maladina to prepare a national oil refinery policy to secure Papua New Guinea’s future energy needs. He said the country has exported oil for 30 years from Kutubu, Moran and Gobe fields, but those reserves are now 85 perc

item.title: Auckland FC wins Inaugural Pro League Title
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Auckland FC has claimed the inaugural OFC Pro League title after defeating South Melbourne FC 2-1 in the final at Eden Park. Goals in each half sealed the historic victory for Auckland, making them the first club to win Oceania’s new professional football league. The two sides entered the decider ev

item.title: Air Niugini opens new sales office in Wewak
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Air Niugini has opened a new sales office in Wewak, East Sepik Province, at the newly built Tangmow Plaza in the Centre of the township. The office was officially opened last week and is intended to improve access to ticketing and travel services for customers in Wewak and surrounding areas. Related

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Fergusson islanders demand compensation for 'unauthorised FCA logging'
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities on Fergusson Island are demanding compensation for environmental damage and unpaid royalties from logging operations they say were carried out without their consent.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Meet some of Papua New Guinea’s most innovative small businesses
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A record number of small businesses entered the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards this year, with the shortlists announced last week in Port Moresby. From the use of artificial intelligence to monitor media reporting to using fly larvae to create poultry feed, we consider the six ingenious projects shortli

item.title: Term 2 Delay in Manus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’
item.description: [Inside PNG] More than half of Australians say they are giving up on regular hobbies such as gardening and playing sport due to stress brought on by extreme weather. The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’ first appeared on Inside PNG . The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-a

item.title: PNG Prime Minister Marape Hails Solomon Islands Prime Minister Election Outcome
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea is ready to deepen cooperation with the Solomon Islands after the election of Matthew Wale as the Pacific nation’s new Prime Minister. Mr Wale was voted into office by Parliament in Honiara on Friday, prompting messages of support from regional leade

item.title: Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea plans to phase out raw log exports by 2025, promoting local processing to boost economic growth, job creation, and sustainable forest management, potentially increasing forestry revenue significantly. The post Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits first appeare

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG's National Airline Air Niugini Begins Gradual Retirement of Fokker Aircraft
item.description: [PNG Facts] Air Niugini has confirmed that its long-serving Fokker aircraft will gradually be withdrawn from service as the airline continues its major refleet program with the introduction of new Airbus A220 jets. Air Niugini Begins Gradual Retirement of Fokker Aircraft The airline’s ongoing fleet replacement

item.title: BFPNG president commends FIBA Oceania high-performance camp initiative
item.description: [Post Courier] The President of the Basketball Federation of Papua New Guinea, David Peter, has acknowledged and thanked FIBA Oceania for hosting the High Performance Camp currently underway on the Gold Coast for Pacific Island nations. The high-performance camp on the Gold Coast started on Saturday, 23 May and wi

item.title: Papua LNG targets 2026 Final Investment Decision as State prepares June/July Development Forum
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] TOTAL Energies has assured the National Government that the Final Investment Decision (FID) for the Papua LNG project will be announced this year, 2026. Prime Minister James Marape revealed at Melanesian House following his return from a bilateral visit with French President Emmanuel Macron last wee

item.title: Buzi villagers launch climate calendar as rising weather changes threaten food sources
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A community-developed seasonal calendar has been launched in Buzi Village in the Torres Strait area of South Fly District, Western Province, as residents respond to the growing impact of climate change on food supplies and natural resources. The calendar was developed by South Fly Rangers in partner

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Bougainville landowners warn companies at Panguna mine to stop work
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A group of landowners in the area of the Panguna mine in Bougainville has issued a public warning to companies trying to redevelop the mine to stop work and leave.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: Papua New Guinea retailers outline substantial capex programs
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Business is back and better than ever for Papua New Guinea's leading retailers after recovering from the post-10 January downturn. As a result, many are now planning substantial investments to support longer-term growth and diversification, as CEOs tell Business Advantage PNG . The post Papua New Gu

item.title: USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has today announced a $F1 million student hardship and bursary support package to protect students and their families The post USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme
item.description: [Inside PNG] Ok Tedi Mining Limited welcomes 44 graduates into its 2026 development program, including seven top scholars from Unitech for a future-ready PNG. The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Developm

item.title: PNG Fuel Retailers and PMV Operators Warned Against Price Hikes Despite Subsidy Delays
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea’s fuel watchdog has moved to calm public concern over fuel prices, warning service station operators and public transport providers not to increase charges beyond approved limits despite delays in government subsidy payments. PNG Fuel Retailers and PMV Operators Warned Against Price

item.title: O’Neill demands accountability on K12 Billion LNG Revenues
item.description: [Post Courier] Ialibu‑Pangia MP Peter O’Neill challenged Prime Minister James Marape in Parliament today, May 26, 2026, to account for the K12 billion already received from the PNG LNG project. O’Neill said the public deserved to know how the money had been used, whether it had been saved or spent, and called for

item.title: PNG Women in Disability Network elects new National Leadership Team PNG
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Papua New Guinea Women in Disability Network (WiDN) has elected a new Board and Executive Committee, marking a significant step in strengthening inclusive leadership and representation of women and girls with disabilities across the country. The elections took place during WiDN’s Annual General

item.title: NGO calls on Forest Minister, board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Community advocacy organization ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minister Salio Waipo and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled. According to evidence given to a Special Parliamentary Committee, the PNG Forest Authority recommended to the Fore

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding one of Papua New Guinea’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: Independence 1 September 2027 :Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting
item.description: [Bougainville News] Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting Sir Jerry Mateparae, Distinguished Moderator, the Honourable James Marape, Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, Foreign Missions, distinguished dignitaries, ladies and gentlemen. First I would … C

item.title: Q&A with PNG Chiefs CEO Lorna McPherson
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's long-awaited entry into the National Rugby League is slowly taking shape. At the centre of the journey is the newly appointed Chief Executive Officer of the PNG Chiefs. She spoke with Michael Philip. The post Q&A with PNG Chiefs CEO Lorna McPherson appeared first on Business Advan

item.title: USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water
item.description: [Education News PNG] As the world marks World Water Day 2026 under the theme “Water and Gender Equality,” research from The University of the South Pacific (USP) has The post USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier
item.description: [Inside PNG] The Autonomous Bougainville Government has launched Bougainville Wings Limited, describing it as Bougainville’s flag carrier and a milestone for the region’s aviation sector. The post ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier first appeared on Inside PNG . The post ABG launches Boug

item.title: PNG PM Marape Apologises Over Fuel Supply Disruptions
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has apologised to citizens following fuel shortages and panic buying experienced across parts of the country over the weekend, saying delays within government payment processes contributed to the situation. Mr Marape said the State has now moved to stabil

item.title: CPL Group and ESPHA enhances Diagnostic Care in East Sepik
item.description: [Post Courier] CPL Group, through the City Pharmacy Medical and Lab Supplies Division, has formalised a key partnership with the East Sepik Provincial Health Authority (ESPHA) through the signing of a Laboratory Equipment Lease Agreement, aimed at strengthening diagnostic services in the province. Under the three-

item.title: Governor Byron Chan commits K1.5 million to empower women through “Mama Bilas Peles Program” in New Ireland
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] New Ireland Governor Byron Chan has reaffirmed his Government’s commitment to empowering women, strengthening grassroots businesses, and driving inclusive economic growth through the official launch of the “Empowering Mama Bilas Peles Program” in Kavieng. In a major announcement during the launching

item.title: PNG Prime Minister meets ATR executives during visit to Aircraft Factory in Toulouse
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape visited the ATR aircraft manufacturing facility in Toulouse, France, on Thursday, where he inspected two of three ATR aircraft being built for Western Province landowners. The visit comes as PNG Air continues expanding its ATR fleet operations in Papua New Guinea. The

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding the KK Connections corporate group, one of PNG’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: PNG PM Heads to France for Climate and Trade Talks with Macron
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has travelled to France for official discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron, with climate change, trade and investment expected to dominate the bilateral agenda. PNG PM Heads to France for Climate and Trade Talks with Macron The visit comes as

item.title: ACT NOW! backs Parliamentary Committee call for immediate and drastic reform in forest sector
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! supports the call from the Special Parliamentary Committee on Public Sector Reform for ‘immediate and nonnegotiable structural, punitive and legislative reforms’ in the forestry sector.

item.title: Court Rejects PNG Deputy PM Rosso's Attempt to Block Investigation
item.description: [PNG Facts] The National Court has dismissed an application by Papua New Guinea Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Lands and Physical Planning John Rosso seeking to halt an ongoing investigation by the Ombudsman Commission into allegations relating to undeclared income. Justice Paulus Dowa delivered the rul
